revision.text:
{{公園 |名称 = 島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘 |英語名称 =YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |画像 =File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |画像キャプション = [[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]] | 国 = {{JPN}} |都市 =〒690-0033
島根県松江市大庭町456 | 緯度度 =35|緯度分 =25|緯度秒 =36.1 | 経度度 =133|経度分 =05|経度秒 =24.2 |分類 = 歴史公園 |公式サイト = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ 八雲立つ風土記の丘公式ページ] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘'''（しまねけんりつやくもたつふどきのおか）は[[島根県]][[松江市]]大庭町にある[[風土記の丘]]（[[歴史公園]]・[[野外博物館]]）施設である。 ==概要== 古代[[出雲国]]の中心地である[[意宇郡]]大庭・竹矢地区には『[[出雲国風土記]]』に登場する[[出雲国府跡]]や出雲[[国分寺]]跡、[[出雲国山代郷遺跡群]]（山代郷正倉跡・北新造院跡）などの古代遺跡群が存在する{{Cite web|title=島根県公式ホームページ|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。八雲立つ風土記の丘は、これらの遺跡を一体的に整備・保存・活用すべく、国の[[風土記の丘]]設置構想に基づいて、1972年（昭和47年）全国で6番目に設置された風土記の丘施設（全国に16箇所設置）である{{Cite web|title=しまね観光ナビ公式ホームページ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。[[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]]のある高台の'''八雲立つ風土記の丘展示学習館'''を中心施設（[[歴史博物館]]）とする。2022年（令和4年）9月9日に開所50周年を迎えた{{Cite web|title=八雲立つ風土記の丘公式ホームページ|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。 ==主要な施設== *八雲立つ風土記の丘展示学習館 *ガイダンス山代の郷 *風土記植物園 ==主要な遺跡== *[[出雲国府跡]] *[[出雲国山代郷遺跡群]] *[[山代二子塚|山代二子塚古墳]] *[[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]] ==詳しく載っている本== *島根県教育委員会『八雲立つ風土記の丘設置事業報告』1973年（昭和48年）3月発行 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 IM-インターネットミュージアム-（八雲立つ風土記の丘）] {{デフォルトソート:しまねけんりつやくもたつふときのおか}} [[Category:島根県の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の公園]]

島根県松江市大庭町456 | 緯度度 =35|緯度分 =25|緯度秒 =36.1 | 経度度 =133|経度分 =05|経度秒 =24.2 |分類 = 歴史公園 |公式サイト = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ 八雲立つ風土記の丘公式ページ] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘'''（しまねけんりつやくもたつふどきのおか）は[[島根県]][[松江市]]大庭町にある[[風土記の丘]]（[[歴史公園]]・[[野外博物館]]）施設である。 ==概要== 古代[[出雲国]]の中心地である[[意宇郡]]大庭・竹矢地区には『[[出雲国風土記]]』に登場する[[出雲国府跡]]や出雲[[国分寺]]跡、[[出雲国山代郷遺跡群]]（山代郷正倉跡・北新造院跡）などの古代遺跡群が存在する{{Cite web|和書|title=島根県公式ホームページ|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。八雲立つ風土記の丘は、これらの遺跡を一体的に整備・保存・活用すべく、国の[[風土記の丘]]設置構想に基づいて、1972年（昭和47年）全国で6番目に設置された風土記の丘施設（全国に16箇所設置）である{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ公式ホームページ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。[[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]]のある高台の'''八雲立つ風土記の丘展示学習館'''を中心施設（[[歴史博物館]]）とする。2022年（令和4年）9月9日に開所50周年を迎えた{{Cite web|和書|title=八雲立つ風土記の丘公式ホームページ|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。 ==主要な施設== *八雲立つ風土記の丘展示学習館 *ガイダンス山代の郷 *風土記植物園 ==主要な遺跡== *[[出雲国府跡]] *[[出雲国山代郷遺跡群]] *[[山代二子塚|山代二子塚古墳]] *[[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]] == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 平所遺跡埴輪窯跡出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は島根県、八雲立つ風土記の丘保管。1977年（昭和52年）6月11日指定。 * 出雲岡田山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は六所神社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1985年（昭和60年）6月6日指定。 === 島根県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 紙本墨書八重垣文書 43通1巻2冊（古文書） - 所有者は個人、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1972年（昭和47年）3月31日指定。 ** 細形銅剣（考古資料） - 所有者は平浜八幡宮、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1964年（昭和39年）5月26日指定。 ** 銅印印文「春」（考古資料） - 所有者は個人、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1968年（昭和43年）6月7日指定。 === 松江市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 小屋谷3号墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1986年（昭和61年）3月31日指定。 ** 熊野銅鐸（考古資料） - 所有者は熊野大社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2001年（平成13年）3月30日指定。 ** 寺床遺跡1号墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2011年（平成23年）7月26日指定。 ==詳しく載っている本== *島根県教育委員会『八雲立つ風土記の丘設置事業報告』1973年（昭和48年）3月発行 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 IM-インターネットミュージアム-（八雲立つ風土記の丘）] {{デフォルトソート:しまねけんりつやくもたつふときのおか}} [[Category:島根県の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の公園]]

所有者は島根県、八雲立つ風土記の丘保管。1977年（昭和52年）6月11日指定。 * 出雲岡田山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は六所神社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1985年（昭和60年）6月6日指定。 === 島根県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 紙本墨書八重垣文書 43通1巻2冊（古文書） - 所有者は個人、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1972年（昭和47年）3月31日指定。 ** 細形銅剣（考古資料） - 所有者は平浜八幡宮、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1964年（昭和39年）5月26日指定。 ** 銅印印文「春」（考古資料） - 所有者は個人、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1968年（昭和43年）6月7日指定。 === 松江市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 小屋谷3号墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1986年（昭和61年）3月31日指定。 ** 熊野銅鐸（考古資料） - 所有者は熊野大社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2001年（平成13年）3月30日指定。 ** 寺床遺跡1号墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2011年（平成23年）7月26日指定。 小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|小屋谷3号墳 四虵鏡 伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|熊野銅鐸 寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡 ==詳しく載っている本== *島根県教育委員会『八雲立つ風土記の丘設置事業報告』1973年（昭和48年）3月発行 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 IM-インターネットミュージアム-（八雲立つ風土記の丘）] {{デフォルトソート:しまねけんりつやくもたつふときのおか}} [[Category:島根県の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の公園]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki-no-Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == 概要 == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the **{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}**, including the **{{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}**, **{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}**, and the **{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}** (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a **national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative** to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in **1972**, it was the **sixth** such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s **central facility** is the **Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center**, located on a hill near the **{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster**. The park celebrated its **50th anniversary** on **September 9, 2022**.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == 文化財 == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts** (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 11, 1977**. * **Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo** (archaeological material) - Owned by **Rokusho Shrine**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 6, 1985**. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Yakumo Shrine Documents** (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1972**. * **Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword** - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **May 26, 1964**. * **Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"** - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 7, 1968**. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1986**. * **Kumano Bronze Bell** - Owned by **{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 30, 2001**. * **Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **July 26, 2011**. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|**Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|**Kumano Bronze Bell** File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|**Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * **Shimane Prefectural Board of Education**, *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published **March 1973**. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki-no-Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == 概要 == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the **{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}**, including the **{{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}**, **{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}**, and the **{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}** (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a **national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative** to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in **1972**, it was the **sixth** such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s **central facility** is the **Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center**, located on a hill near the **{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster**. The park celebrated its **50th anniversary** on **September 9, 2022**.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == 文化財 == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts** (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 11, 1977**. * **Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo** (archaeological material) - Owned by **Rokusho Shrine**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 6, 1985**. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Yakumo Shrine Documents** (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1972**. * **Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword** - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **May 26, 1964**. * **Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"** - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 7, 1968**. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1986**. * **Kumano Bronze Bell** - Owned by **{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 30, 2001**. * **Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **July 26, 2011**. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|**Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|**Kumano Bronze Bell** File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|**Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * **Shimane Prefectural Board of Education**, *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published **March 1973**. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]]

File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|**Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|**Kumano Bronze Bell** File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|**Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * **Shimane Prefectural Board of Education**, *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published **March 1973**. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki-no-Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == 概要 == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the **{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}**, including the **{{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}**, **{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}**, and the **{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}** (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a **national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative** to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in **1972**, it was the **sixth** such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s **central facility** is the **Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center**, located on a hill near the **{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster**. The park celebrated its **50th anniversary** on **September 9, 2022**.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == 文化財 == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts** (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 11, 1977**. * **Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo** (archaeological material) - Owned by **Rokusho Shrine**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 6, 1985**. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Yakumo Shrine Documents** (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1972**. * **Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword** - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **May 26, 1964**. * **Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"** - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 7, 1968**. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1986**. * **Kumano Bronze Bell** - Owned by **{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 30, 2001**. * **Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **July 26, 2011**. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|**Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|**Kumano Bronze Bell** File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|**Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * **Shimane Prefectural Board of Education**, *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published **March 1973**. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]]

revision.text:
{{公園 |名称 = 島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘 |英語名称 =YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |画像 =File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |画像キャプション = [[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]] | 国 = {{JPN}} |都市 =〒690-0033
島根県松江市大庭町456 | 緯度度 =35|緯度分 =25|緯度秒 =36.1 | 経度度 =133|経度分 =05|経度秒 =24.2 |分類 = 歴史公園 |公式サイト = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ 八雲立つ風土記の丘公式ページ] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘'''（しまねけんりつやくもたつふどきのおか）は[[島根県]][[松江市]]大庭町にある[[風土記の丘]]（[[歴史公園]]・[[野外博物館]]）施設である。 ==概要== 古代[[出雲国]]の中心地である[[意宇郡]]大庭・竹矢地区には『[[出雲国風土記]]』に登場する[[出雲国府跡]]や出雲[[国分寺]]跡、[[出雲国山代郷遺跡群]]（山代郷正倉跡・北新造院跡）などの古代遺跡群が存在する{{Cite web|和書|title=島根県公式ホームページ|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。八雲立つ風土記の丘は、これらの遺跡を一体的に整備・保存・活用すべく、国の[[風土記の丘]]設置構想に基づいて、1972年（昭和47年）全国で6番目に設置された風土記の丘施設（全国に16箇所設置）である{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ公式ホームページ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。[[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]]のある高台の'''八雲立つ風土記の丘展示学習館'''を中心施設（[[歴史博物館]]）とする。2022年（令和4年）9月9日に開所50周年を迎えた{{Cite web|和書|title=八雲立つ風土記の丘公式ホームページ|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}}。 ==主要な施設== *八雲立つ風土記の丘展示学習館 *ガイダンス山代の郷 *風土記植物園 ==主要な遺跡== *[[出雲国府跡]] *[[出雲国山代郷遺跡群]] *[[山代二子塚|山代二子塚古墳]] *[[岡田山古墳|岡田山古墳群]] == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 平所遺跡埴輪窯跡出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は島根県、八雲立つ風土記の丘保管。1977年（昭和52年）6月11日指定。 * 出雲岡田山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は六所神社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1985年（昭和60年）6月6日指定。 平所遺跡 見返りの鹿埴輪.JPG|平所遺跡 見返りの鹿埴輪 平所遺跡 家形埴輪.JPG|平所遺跡 家形埴輪 平所遺跡 馬形埴輪.JPG|平所遺跡 馬形埴輪 平所遺跡 人物埴輪.JPG|平所遺跡 人物埴輪 === 島根県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 紙本墨書八重垣文書 43通1巻2冊（古文書） - 所有者は個人、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1972年（昭和47年）3月31日指定。 ** 細形銅剣（考古資料） - 所有者は平浜八幡宮、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1964年（昭和39年）5月26日指定。 ** 銅印印文「春」（考古資料） - 所有者は個人、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1968年（昭和43年）6月7日指定。 === 松江市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 小屋谷3号墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1986年（昭和61年）3月31日指定。 ** 熊野銅鐸（考古資料） - 所有者は熊野大社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2001年（平成13年）3月30日指定。 ** 寺床遺跡1号墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2011年（平成23年）7月26日指定。 小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|小屋谷3号墳 四虵鏡 伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|熊野銅鐸 寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡 ==詳しく載っている本== *島根県教育委員会『八雲立つ風土記の丘設置事業報告』1973年（昭和48年）3月発行 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 IM-インターネットミュージアム-（八雲立つ風土記の丘）] {{デフォルトソート:しまねけんりつやくもたつふときのおか}} [[Category:島根県の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の公園]]

所有者は島根県、八雲立つ風土記の丘保管。1977年（昭和52年）6月11日指定。 * 出雲岡田山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は六所神社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1985年（昭和60年）6月6日指定。 平所遺跡 家形埴輪.JPG|平所遺跡 家形埴輪 平所遺跡 馬形埴輪.JPG|平所遺跡 馬形埴輪 平所遺跡 人物埴輪.JPG|平所遺跡 人物埴輪 === 島根県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 紙本墨書八重垣文書 43通1巻2冊（古文書） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。1986年（昭和61年）3月31日指定。 ** 熊野銅鐸（考古資料） - 所有者は熊野大社、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2001年（平成13年）3月30日指定。 ** 寺床遺跡1号墳出土品（考古資料） - 所有者は松江市、八雲立つ風土記の丘寄託。2011年（平成23年）7月26日指定。 小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|小屋谷3号墳 四虵鏡 伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|熊野銅鐸 寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡 ==詳しく載っている本== *島根県教育委員会『八雲立つ風土記の丘設置事業報告』1973年（昭和48年）3月発行 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 IM-インターネットミュージアム-（八雲立つ風土記の丘）] {{デフォルトソート:しまねけんりつやくもたつふときのおか}} [[Category:島根県の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の公園]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki-no-Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == 概要 == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the **{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}**, including the **{{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}**, **{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}**, and the **{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}** (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a **national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative** to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in **1972**, it was the **sixth** such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s **central facility** is the **Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center**, located on a hill near the **{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster**. The park celebrated its **50th anniversary** on **September 9, 2022**.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == 文化財 == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts** (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 11, 1977**. * **Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo** (archaeological material) - Owned by **Rokusho Shrine**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 6, 1985**. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Yakumo Shrine Documents** (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1972**. * **Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword** - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **May 26, 1964**. * **Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"** - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **June 7, 1968**. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 31, 1986**. * **Kumano Bronze Bell** - Owned by **{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}**, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **March 30, 2001**. * **Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on **July 26, 2011**. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|**Koyadani Kofun No.3** - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|**Kumano Bronze Bell** File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|**Teradoko Kofun No.1** - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * **Shimane Prefectural Board of Education**, *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published **March 1973**. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki-no-Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == 概要 == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == 文化財 == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == 概要 == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == 文化財 == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == 概要 == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == 文化財 == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 文化財 ==" to "== Cultural Properties =="
revision.text:
[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''.
