revision.text:
[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|出雲国分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|出雲国府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|出雲国山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. * '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. * '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' * '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. * '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. * '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. revision.text:
[[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社|12=draft}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]]

{{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=draft}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=draft}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=draft}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=draft}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社|12=draft}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]]

{{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=draft}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=draft}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=draft}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=draft}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社|12=draft}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=draft}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=draft}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=draft}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=draft}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社|12=draft}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=draft}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=draft}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=draft}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=draft}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|12=draft}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社|12=draft}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox park |name = Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka |english_name = YAKUMOTATSU FUDOKI-NO-OKA Park |image = File:岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG |caption = {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} cluster | country = {{JPN}} |location = 〒690-0033
{{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shimane Prefecture, Ōba-chō 456 | lat_deg =35| lat_min =25| lat_sec =36.1 | lon_deg =133| lon_min =05| lon_sec =24.2 |type = Historical Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|ja|岡田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|ja|熊野大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%AB%8B%E3%81%A4%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[en:Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill]]
