[[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]], Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iu District, Izumo Province|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11073594}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山代二子塚古墳|1=Yamashiro Futagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466267}} * [[:en:Okadayama Kofun|Okadayama Kofun]] cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''[[:en:Kumano Taisha|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)]]''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘|version=103854340|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Shimane Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yakumotatsu Fudoki no Oka}} {{wikidata link|Q104721896}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in [[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]], Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iu District, Izumo Province|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11073594}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山代二子塚古墳|1=Yamashiro Futagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466267}} * [[:en:Okadayama Kofun|Okadayama Kofun]] cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''[[:en:Kumano Taisha|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)]]''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山代二子塚古墳|1=Yamashiro Futagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466267}} * [[:en:Okadayama Kofun|Okadayama Kofun]] cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''[[:en:Kumano Taisha|Kumano Shrine (Izumo)]]''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘|version=103854340|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] {{Commons category|Yakumotatsu Fudoki no Oka}} {{wikidata link|Q104721896}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

'''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue, Shimane}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iu District, Izumo Province|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11073594}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster'''. Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山代二子塚古墳|1=Yamashiro Futagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466267}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Taisha|en|Kumano Taisha|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Shrine (Izumo)}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘|version=103854340|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] {{Commons category|Yakumotatsu Fudoki no Oka}} {{wikidata link|Q104721896}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

'''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue, Shimane}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iu District, Izumo Province|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11073594}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山代二子塚古墳|1=Yamashiro Futagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466267}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Taisha|en|Kumano Taisha|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Shrine (Izumo)}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘|version=103854340|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] {{Commons category|Yakumotatsu Fudoki no Oka}} {{wikidata link|Q104721896}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Park |website = [https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/ Official Website] }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|25|36.1|N|133|05|24.2|E}}}} '''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue, Shimane|1=Matsue}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iu District, Izumo Province|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11073594}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', '''{{ill|Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokubun-ji Temple Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun|1=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山代二子塚古墳|1=Yamashiro Futagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466267}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun|1=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Taisha|en|Kumano Taisha|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|1=Kumano Taisha}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national Fudoki-no-Oka preservation initiative''' to protect, develop, and utilize these ancient sites. Opened in '''1972''', it was the '''sixth''' such facility in Japan (out of a total of 16 nationwide).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Tourism Official Website|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20351|accessdate=2022-11-26}} The park’s '''central facility''' is the '''Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center''', located on a hill near the '''{{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun|1=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster'''. The park celebrated its '''50th anniversary''' on '''September 9, 2022'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Official Website of Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka|url=https://www.yakumotatu-fudokinooka.jp/|accessdate=2022-11-26}} == 主要な施設 == * Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition and Learning Center * Guidance Center "Yamashiro-no-Sato" * Fudoki Botanical Garden == 主要な遺跡 == * {{ill|Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja|山代二子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Futagotsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山代二子塚古墳|1=Yamashiro Futagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466267}} * {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|en|Okadayama Kofun|WD=Q11473525|lt=Okadayama Kofun|1=Okadayama Kofun}} cluster == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Hirasho Site Haniwa Kiln Artifacts''' (archaeological material) - Owned by Shimane Prefecture, stored at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 11, 1977'''. * '''Artifacts from Okadayama Kofun, Izumo''' (archaeological material) - Owned by '''Rokusho Shrine''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 6, 1985'''. === Shimane Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Yakumo Shrine Documents''' (43 documents, 1 scroll, 2 books) - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1972'''. ** '''Narrow-Bladed Bronze Sword''' - Owned by Hirahama Hachiman Shrine, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''May 26, 1964'''. ** '''Bronze Seal Inscribed with "Spring"''' - Owned by a private individual, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''June 7, 1968'''. === Matsue City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Artifacts from Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 31, 1986'''. ** '''Kumano Bronze Bell''' - Owned by '''{{ill|Kumano Taisha|en|Kumano Taisha|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Shrine (Izumo)|1=Kumano Taisha}}''', deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''March 30, 2001'''. ** '''Artifacts from Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Owned by Matsue City, deposited at Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka. Designated on '''July 26, 2011'''. File:小屋谷3号墳 四ち鏡.JPG|'''Koyadani Kofun No.3''' - Four Snakes Mirror File:伝熊野 銅鐸.JPG|'''Kumano Bronze Bell''' File:寺床1号墳 斜縁神獣鏡.JPG|'''Teradoko Kofun No.1''' - Slant-Edged Sacred Beast Mirror == 詳しく載っている本 == * '''Shimane Prefectural Board of Education''', *Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka Establishment Report*, published '''March 1973'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.museum.or.jp/museum/6026 Internet Museum - Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘|version=103854340|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘]] {{Commons category|Yakumotatsu Fudoki no Oka}} {{wikidata link|Q104721896}} [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

'''Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka''' (島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (historical park and open-air museum) located in {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue, Shimane|1=Matsue}}, Ōba-chō, Shimane Prefecture. == Overview == The Ōba-Takeya area of {{ill|Iu District, Izumo Province|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iu District, Izumo Province|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11073594}}, which was the center of ancient {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contains many significant archaeological sites mentioned in the '''{{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', including the '''{{ill|Izumo Kokufu|lt=Izumo Kokufu Ruins|ja|Izumo Province Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', and the '''{{ill|Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja|Izumo Province 山代郷遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kuni Yamashiro-gō Site Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Yamashiro-gō Shōsō Ruins and Kitashin Zōin Ruins).{{Cite web|和書|title=Shimane Prefecture Official Website|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/yakumotatuhudokinooka.html|accessdate=2022-11-26}} Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-Oka was established as part of a '''national
